name: git-code-audit
author: Bill Welense
abstract: Analyze a specific part of a repository through git history first, then selectively read hotspot files to explain who works there, what has changed, where bugs cluster, and what code is worth reading next.

patterns.note: Single SKILL.md with inline command patterns, no separate pattern files

triggers: Tell me who works on this part of the code
Analyze what changed in a directory
Audit a file or folder through git history
Find churn hotspots in a specific path
Show bug hotspots for a code area
Read hotspot files after analyzing git history

git-history-analysis.description: Use git history to understand ownership, churn, and risk before reading code

code-ownership.description: Identify who contributes to a scoped code area and how ownership is concentrated

hotspot-analysis.description: Correlate churn, bug-fix history, and firefighting signals to find risky files

selective-code-reading.description: Read only the most relevant hotspot files after git-first analysis

references.title: The Git Commands I Run Before Reading Any Code
